channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: Spain leave it late to book semifinal date with France
item.description: Mikel Merino comes off the bench to break Belgian hearts after slip from sub keeper Senne Lammens.

item.title: Dozens of abducted schoolchildren and teachers rescued in Nigeria
item.description: The hostages were taken from three separate schools in the southwestern state of Oyo in May.

item.title: Lebanon historic sites destroyed by Israeli strikes
item.description: Israeli air strikes on southern Lebanon have caused catastrophic destruction and damaged historic landmarks.

item.title: How Wong Kim Ark’s legacy reignited the fight for birthright citizenship
item.description: Sandra Wong did not know her great-grandfather growing up. But his legacy would catapult her into the public eye.

item.title: Ukrainian attacks cause chaos at fuel stations across Russia
item.description: Ukraine’s targeting of Russia’s major oil refineries has sparked a nationwide fuel shortage.

item.title: Donald Trump removes final members of independent US election commission
item.description: The dismissals leave the federal election body vacant as Trump presses for broader changes to US voting rules.

item.title: Egypt World Cup team return home to hero’s welcome after historic run
item.description: Egypt’s squad has returned home to a hero’s welcome following their controversial exit from the World Cup.

item.title: Why are politicians and sanctioned states embracing crypto?
item.description: Bitcoin is falling, yet cryptocurrency is spreading beyond investing into politics, sanctions and global finance.

item.title: What happened at France v Morocco
item.description: France faced Morocco in a rematch of the 2022 World Cup semi-final.

item.title: EU says ‘addictive’ features on Instagram and Facebook breach its rules
item.description: Bloc says some design features on platforms built by Meta can cause users' brains to go into 'autopilot mode'.

item.title: Witnesses contest the Trump administration’s account of Texas ICE killing
item.description: Those present say Lorenzo Salgado Araujo did not ram or 'weaponise' his vehicle, as the US government claimed.

item.title: Can the agreement between Iran and the US be rescued?
item.description: Latest attacks jeopardise ceasefire and memorandum of understanding.

item.title: Eliminated Morocco turn attention to cohosting 2030 World Cup
item.description: Morocco were eliminated from the 2026 World Cup by a 2-0 defeat by France in the quarterfinals, but host next edition.

item.title: Sinner demolishes Djokovic to set up Wimbledon final with Zverev
item.description: Sinner is targeting his fifth Grand Slam title, which would bring him within two of the tally of rival Carlos Alcaraz.

item.title: Indian cricket board to review T20 team’s ‘bad phase’ after England defeats
item.description: India slumped to a nine-wicket defeat in the fourth T20 against England, following a 2-0 series whitewash in Ireland.

item.title: UK police arrest man suspected of murdering former MP Ann Widdecombe
item.description: Reform UK politician Widdecombe, 78, was found dead at her home on Thursday with serious injuries.

item.title: Peru’s President-elect Fujimori says she wants to restore ties with Mexico
item.description: Keiko Fujimori has 'every intention' of ending months-long rift with Mexico over its political asylum decisions.

item.title: Hospital workers wounded in Israeli drone attack on Gaza’s Kamal Adwan
item.description: Palestinian Health Ministry denounces attack as part of Israel's 'systematic targeting of health facilities'.

item.title: Ronaldo still has Portugal future if he wants it, says new coach Jesus
item.description: The 2026 World Cup exit for Portugal was believed to be the end of Cristiano Ronaldo's international career.

item.title: FIFA has nearly 1,200 tickets still on sale for World Cup final at $7,380
item.description: Several tickets remain for lower decks ranging from $19,995 to $32,970, as well as Trophy Lounge section at $34,500.

item.title: Russian attacks kill five as Ukraine continues to target oil infrastructure
item.description: Ukrainian officials say Russia's attack on Kramatorsk kills four, while a strike on Zaporizhzhia kills one.

item.title: Hundreds give hero’s welcome to Egypt after best World Cup run
item.description: Supporters waved Egyptian and Palestinian flags in support of Gaza as the Pharaohs boarded an open-top bus for a parade.

item.title: Trump hints at further Iran negotiations after exchange of fire over Hormuz
item.description: Iran denies it requested new talks with US as new attacks threaten the full resumption of war.

item.title: Who is Fadel Shaker? Lebanese pop star released on bail
item.description: In 2012, singing star Shaker quit his music career, before being accused of joining an armed group in Lebanon.

item.title: Ruthless Zverev spoils Fery’s Wimbledon dream to power into final
item.description: Alexander Zverev will face reigning Wimbledon champion Jannik Sinner or seven-time winner Novak ​Djokovic in the final.
